title: hieracium lagophyton
side_effects: Hieracium lagophyton can have side effects if used improperly, such as causing gastrointestinal issues, including nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea, due to its high content of sesquiterpene lactones. Prolonged exposure to the plant's extracts can also lead to allergic reactions, such as skin irritation, itching, and swelling, particularly in individuals with sensitive skin. In rare cases, improper use of Hieracium lagophyton may result in more severe side effects, including increased heart rate and blood pressure, which can be a concern for people with pre-existing cardiovascular conditions. Additionally, consuming large quantities of the plant's extracts may interact with certain medications, such as blood thinners and diabetes medications.
